sources.label: Daikin Global press releases 2026 (no items since June 6; latest relevant: May 21 Poland heat-pump plant, May 11 Delta Electronics data-centre cooling MOU, May 12 FUSION30/results)
Daikin outlines U.S. growth strategy / data-center cooling pitch at J.P. Morgan Industrials Conference
Simply Wall St: Daikin targets data centers with new cooling systems (TSE:6367)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new fundamental evidence since June 6 and only a -0.75% drift day; yesterday's hold stands. Mild negative delta because the data-center cooling story is increasingly consensus (FUSION30 plan, JPM Industrials pitch, dedicated DC unit) at a $42B cap, so residual alpha is eroding without a new catalyst rather than the rank changing.
verdicts.bull: Daikin is converting from commodity HVAC to an integrated thermal-infrastructure partner for hyperscale AI data centers (Chilldyne liquid-cooling acquisition, Delta MOU in Asia-Oceania, modular large-tonnage capacity), with the North American DC cooling business projected to more than triple by 2030.
verdicts.neutral: At ~$42B market cap and +14.9% YTD, the data-center optionality is partially priced; residential/commercial HVAC cyclicality still dominates near-term earnings, so the stock drifts with macro until DC cooling orders show up in segment numbers.
verdicts.bear: Data-center cooling is a crowded knife fight (Vertiv, Schneider, Johnson Controls, Trane, LG) where Daikin is a late scaler, and a global HVAC demand slowdown or US housing weakness could swamp the DC narrative while the YTD rerating unwinds.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q1 FY26 results and first FUSION30 progress update quantifying data-center cooling orders/backlog, or a named hyperscaler design win for the Chilldyne/Applied liquid-cooling stack.
verdicts.primaryRisk: DC cooling revenue ramps slower than the narrative while core HVAC (US residential, China) decelerates, compressing the multiple gained in the YTD rerating.
